meaning: vt.切开,撕裂n.裂缝,狭长切口
examples: She watched them through a slit in the curtains.
He began to slit open each envelope.
He slit open the envelope and took out the letter.
Or it could be because some restaurants cut a slit down the top, nearly slicing it in half.
They had heard his plane and were safely in a slit trench when the bombs fell.
